_comment: Optional airband receiver config for maia-httpd. Copy to /root/airband.json on the Pluto. If absent, maia-httpd uses its built-in defaults, now identical to this file (including gain_db 48). samp_rate MUST be the value the channelizer was built for (audio rate = samp_rate / 128 / 7 = 15625 sps at 14 MHz). channels_hz: up to 21 absolute channel centers, all within center_hz +/- samp_rate/2. gain: default fixed manual 48 dB -- the bare-front-end clipping knee (71 dB clips ~13-15% of the wideband ADC -> broadband intermod / raised floor) that ALSO lowers the prominence of the conducted on-board spur comb, which is amplified by RX gain (firmware/diagnostics/term_tests.py). Raise toward the 71-73 dB AD9361 ceiling (range [-1 1 73]) ONLY behind an external selective filter at a quiet site (0 clipping -> max sensitivity for weak/distant channels); lower further (~40) to suppress the comb more, trading weak-signal sensitivity. The conducted spur comb (incl. 120 MHz = 40 MHz ref 3rd harmonic) is internal hardware -- lower gain reduces it but a clean supply / external reference is the real fix (firmware/diagnostics/).
